question:
Why is the expectation that employees remain perpetually cheerful at work often critiqued?
A) Cheerfulness makes coworkers uncomfortable.
B) It is a form of emotional labor that can suppress authentic expression and create undue psychological strain.
C) Happiness has been shown to reduce productivity by 90%.
D) Most employment contracts legally prohibit positive emotions.
